specific_location: Blue River Arm of Dillion Reservoir, CO
description: The bird was observed foraging along a rocky shoreline and ice edge, occasionally working its way up a small creek where it would become obscured by snow. This was a rather stout and rotund shorebird similar in size to a Killdeer. Its legs were short and orange, and the bill was tapered with a slight droop near the end. Bill color was dark with a dull orange base. There was a fair amount of streaking and spotting on the lower part of its breast and flanks. Head and neck was gray with a small amount of white around the eye, while the back was more of a grayish-brown. The narrow white stripe on the posterior part of its wing was visible when it made short flights along the shoreline.
photographer: Photographer: Jack Bushong, Jack Bushong, Glenn Walbek
